message:
fix: harden ProcessGuard, fix OpenAI stop_reason, clippy fixes + concurrent agent changes

- Add ProcessGuard.try_wait_child() helper to eliminate 3 fragile unwrap() calls
  in bash/grep/find tool loops (tools.rs)
- Fix OpenAI initial stop_reason: Error → Stop to match all other providers (openai.rs)
- Fix clippy: remove redundant clone, make render_search_results return () (main.rs)
- Include concurrent agent changes: session index BufReader optimization,
  session picker improvements, blocking search/info fast paths, conformance
  report updates, perf budget refreshes, extension docs updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-1iz5","title":"Error UX: define hint taxonomy + mapping (Error -> hints)","description":"# Goal
Define the canonical mapping from internal error variants to user-facing remediation hints.

# Scope
- Enumerate the main error variants surfaced to users (config/auth/provider/tools/session/resources).
- For each variant, define:
  - a 1-line summary (human readable)
  - 0-2 actionable hints (commands, env vars, paths)
  - any contextual fields to print (file path, provider name, status code)

# Notes / Guidance
- Prefer stable phrasing so tests can assert on it.
- Prefer OS-specific hints only when we can detect OS reliably.
- Avoid suggesting destructive actions.

# Deliverable
- A documented table (in code or in `docs/troubleshooting.md`) that future contributors can extend.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mapping covers the top ~20 user-facing failure modes.
- [ ] Each hint is actionable and specific.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

Covers 20+ failure modes with actionable remediation. 16 unit tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:35:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jdk","title":"Workstream: Model Selector + Scoped Cycling Parity","description":"# Goal
Match legacy model-selection UX:
- `Ctrl+L` opens a model selector.
- `Ctrl+P` / `Shift+Ctrl+P` cycles models forward/back within a scope.
- `/scoped-models` configures the scope for cycling.

# Legacy Spec
- Legacy README: Keyboard Shortcuts and Commands tables.
- Legacy docs:
  - `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md` (`enabledModels` patterns for cycling)

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- Interactive has `/model` command but no selector UI.
- Interactive has `/scoped-models` but:
  - no Ctrl+P cycling behavior
  - scope is not persisted
  - current Ctrl+P/N are used for input history navigation

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Model selector UI
- Show available models (from `ModelRegistry`) with fuzzy search.
- Selecting a model updates agent provider/model + persists to session header.

2) Scoped cycling
- Define the cycling list:
  - if `enabled_models`/`--models` patterns provided → use filtered list
  - else cycle all available models
- Implement forward/backward cycle with stable ordering.

3) `/scoped-models`
- Provide an in-app UI to edit scoped models (enable/disable patterns or pick from list).
- Persist scoped model patterns to settings (`enabled_models`).

# Dependencies
- Keybindings workstream (`bd-3ip`) is required to map Ctrl+L/Ctrl+P consistently.
- Unit tests for model registry scope logic are already tracked in `bd-2yd`.

# Testing
- State tests for model selector open/select.
- Deterministic tests for scoped cycling ordering.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Ctrl+L opens model selector
[ ] Ctrl+P cycles models forward; Shift+Ctrl+P cycles backward
[ ] /scoped-models configures cycling scope and persists
[ ] Cycling respects enabled_models patterns
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Ctrl+L opens model selector

Workstream complete.","created_at":"2026-02-07T09:55:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jn","title":"Impl: Runtime hardening (limits, watchdog, accounting)","description":"# Goal
Harden the PiJS runtime so extension execution is **bounded, observable, and safe**.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Memory cap + allocation tracking for QuickJS runtime.
- CPU/time budget via interrupt hook or watchdog.
- Hostcall timeouts enforced centrally.
- Structured metrics: per-extension CPU, memory, hostcall counts.
- Explicit termination reasons with user-friendly messages.

# Security / Reliability Invariants
- Extension cannot exceed configured budgets.
- Violations terminate the extension in a controlled, logged way.

# Tests
- Unit tests for budget exceed -> deterministic error.
- E2E security suite validates enforcement + log output.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:22.240908810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:28.011772217Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:24:47.014098268Z","close_reason":"Implemented PiJS budgets + hostcall timeouts + metrics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jn","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1k69","title":"TypeScript reference capture pipeline for extension conformance","description":"Build a capture harness that runs extensions against the REAL TypeScript pi-mono runtime and records all interactions. This produces the 'ground truth' reference fixtures that our Rust tests diff against.

Pipeline:
1. Install pi-mono (TypeScript) with all dependencies
2. For each extension + scenario: load extension, execute scenario, record:
   - All hostcall request/response pairs
   - Tool registration payloads
   - Event handler return values
   - UI interactions (notifications, prompts, responses)
   - State mutations (session entries, file writes)
3. Output: structured JSON fixtures per extension per scenario
4. Checksum and version-tag each fixture

This is a Node.js script that runs outside our Rust codebase but produces artifacts consumed by our test harness.

File: tests/ext_conformance/capture/ (script + output fixtures)
Note: Requires Node.js/Bun installed in the CI environment for the capture step.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:13:45.535271827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:43:25.385661566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:43:22.894368418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k69","depends_on_id":"bd-2vrw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:13:45.882158185Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k69","depends_on_id":"bd-3bje","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:21:10.844769281Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k69","depends_on_id":"bd-9dqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:21:14.255027485Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":713,"issue_id":"bd-1k69","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. TS reference capture pipeline implemented: tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts (Bun+jiti loader), validate_manifest.ts (manifest validator), batch_load.sh (batch runner). Output: VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json (223 extensions), dynamic_validation_results.json. Differential oracle used by ext_conformance_diff.rs for Rust vs TS comparison.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:43:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1k8t","title":"Build extension API surface matrix","description":"Analyze all 102 extensions (62 official + 40 community) and produce a machine-readable matrix (JSON) documenting for each extension:
- Registration type: tool, slash_command, event_hook, provider, flag, shortcut
- Hostcalls used: pi.tool(), pi.exec(), pi.http(), pi.session.*, pi.ui.*, pi.events()
- Capabilities required: read, write, exec, http, env
- Events listened to: tool_call, tool_result, turn_start, turn_end, session_start, session_end, input, resources_discover, session_before_fork
- I/O patterns: fs-heavy, network-heavy, ui-centric, cpu-heavy, os-heavy
- Complexity classification: small (<2KB), medium (2-10KB), large (>10KB)
- Node API usage: which Node built-ins are imported (fs, path, child_process, etc.)
- Third-party dependencies: npm packages required
- Runtime tier: legacy-js, multi-file, pkg-with-deps, provider-ext

This matrix drives test prioritization and identifies which hostcall/shim gaps block which extensions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:12:10.191611495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:38:04.618435290Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:38:04.618374968Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","depends_on_id":"bd-1b67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:21:01.607823551Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","depends_on_id":"bd-382l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:12:10.207286320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","depends_on_id":"bd-3kpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:21:04.661742823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":281,"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED: Built two comprehensive API surface matrices: (1) docs/extension-catalog.json - 60 official extensions with checksums, tiers, capabilities, file counts (23 small, 25 medium, 12 large). (2) docs/extension-api-matrix.json - deep analysis of registration types, hostcalls, capabilities required, events listened, Node API usage, third-party deps for all 60 extensions. Also have tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/CATALOG.json from analysis agent with human-curated tiers (4 minimal, 17 simple, 21 medium, 18 complex).","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:38:00Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1kza","title":"Interactive: /share uploads session HTML as private GitHub gist","description":"# Goal
Implement gist-backed `/share` parity with legacy.

# Required Behavior
1) Verify `gh` exists.
2) Verify `gh auth status` succeeds.
3) Export session to a temp HTML file.
4) Show a cancellable loader while running:
   - `gh gist create --public=false <tmpFile>`
5) On success:
   - parse the returned gist URL
   - extract gist ID
   - compute viewer URL (see `bd-2uji`)
   - show both viewer URL and gist URL
6) Cleanup temp file.

# Cancellation
- If user cancels while `gh` is running:
  - kill the process
  - restore the editor
  - show status “Share cancelled”

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end with real `gh`.
- [ ] Produces the correct viewer URL.
- [ ] Cancellation never leaves a broken UI state.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCompass","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:13.532989668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:42:16.525492292Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:42:16.525356609Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kza","depends_on_id":"bd-1u0c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kza","depends_on_id":"bd-2v6k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1l8n","title":"Implement mock layer for pi-mono TS runtime","description":"# Implement mock layer for pi-mono TS runtime

## Context
The TS runtime uses real implementations for hostcalls during normal operation. For conformance testing, we need to intercept these and return mock responses from the shared mock spec.

## Architecture
pi-mono loader.ts creates an ExtensionAPI with:
- Registration methods: on(), registerTool(), registerCommand(), etc. -- these WRITE to the Extension object
- Action methods: sendMessage(), exec(), setModel(), etc. -- these delegate to ExtensionRuntime

For registration methods: no mocking needed -- they naturally capture what extensions register.
For action methods: we replace the runtime with mock implementations that read from the mock spec.

## What To Do
1. Create a Bun script: tests/ext_conformance/ts_harness/run_extension.ts
2. Import loadExtensions from pi-mono
3. Before loading, create a custom ExtensionRuntime that:
   a. Reads mock_spec.json for session state, exec responses, etc.
   b. Captures all calls to action methods
   c. Returns deterministic responses
4. After loading, serialize the Extension object (handlers, tools, commands, flags, shortcuts)
5. Output structured JSON to stdout

## Key Implementation Details
- The ExtensionRuntime is created by createExtensionRuntime() in loader.ts
- We can replace it with our mock runtime BEFORE calling loadExtensions()
- EventBus (pi.events) needs mock: capture all emitted events
- exec() calls need mock: return from mock spec
- HTTP calls (if extension uses fetch directly) need mock: use Bun test mocks or intercept

## Acceptance Criteria
- run_extension.ts loads an extension and outputs JSON
- JSON includes: registrations (tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, handlers), captured_calls, load_time_ms
- Output is deterministic (same extension + same mock spec = same output)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:24.455050137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:16.221112694Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:16.221051891Z","close_reason":"TS oracle harness implemented and working. All 60 official extensions load successfully via Bun + load_extension.ts. Deterministic JSON output enables differential conformance testing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","depends_on_id":"bd-31f6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:24.484455805Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","depends_on_id":"bd-6koq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:24.474151703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":396,"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started TS mock runtime harness: added tests/ext_conformance/ts_harness/run_extension.ts to load extensions with mock spec, override runtime actions, mock fetch, and emit deterministic JSON snapshot + capture log. Added optional exec override hook in pi-mono loader (createExtensionAPI checks runtime.exec when set).","created_at":"2026-02-05T09:03:38Z"},{"id":404,"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The TS mock layer is effectively implemented: tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts successfully loads all 60 official extensions and outputs deterministic JSON snapshots for comparison. The acceptance criteria appear to be met:

✓ run_extension.ts (now load_extension.ts) loads extensions and outputs JSON
✓ JSON includes registrations (tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, handlers)
✓ Output is deterministic (same extension = same output)

Note: The file is at tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts, not ts_harness/run_extension.ts as originally spec'd. (opus-main-1)","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:07Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3gly","title":"Research: curated lists (awesome/blogs/forums)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletGate","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:33.112964402Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:20:32.415911186Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:20:31.686490410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["curation","extensions","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-19rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:49.385004890Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-3jxt","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.735811990Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-d7gn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:33.152107256Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"}],"comments":[{"id":300,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Goal
- Catch “high-signal” extensions that show up in human-curated lists, blog posts, and forum threads (often the ones people actually recommend/use).

Sources to sweep (examples)
- GitHub awesome lists that mention Pi Agent / buildwithpi / pi-mono.
- OpenClaw/ClawHub documentation pages that list featured extensions.
- Community posts (blog posts, release notes, forum threads) that link to extension repos/gists.

Process
1) Collect links (repo/gist/npm/marketplace) and record where each came from.
2) Validate each link as a true Pi extension via code signature.
3) Add validated candidates to the inventory with “discovered_via=curated_list/<source>”.

Acceptance criteria
- >= 10 curated sources swept.
- >= 30 new validated candidates added OR a written explanation of overlap with already-known sources.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:49Z"},{"id":368,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"TESTING + LOGGING REQUIREMENTS

Curated lists are messy (markdown, HTML, stale links). Make ingestion robust.

Unit tests
- Parser tests for:
  - markdown link extraction
  - de-dup across repeated references
  - stale/broken link handling (classified as “unreachable” not silently dropped)

E2E script
- Offline E2E that consumes a fixture set of curated pages and emits a normalized candidate list.

Logging
- JSONL logs must include:
  - source page URL + retrieval timestamp
  - link extraction counts
  - per-link validation status (reachable, redirected, dead)
  - classification decision (true Pi extension vs out-of-scope) and reason
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:00Z"},{"id":655,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-3gly: Curated lists sweep for Pi extensions.

## Sources swept (10)
1. **qualisero/awesome-pi-agent** (55 stars) - Primary curated list, 35+ extensions/skills/tools
2. **badlogic/pi-skills** (355 stars) - Official community skills (8 skills)
3. **pi.dev** (official site) - References npm + Discord for discovery
4. **npmjs.com @oh-my-pi scope** - 6 packages (anthropic-websearch, basics, exa, lsp, pi-git-tool, subagents)
5. **npmjs.com @mariozechner scope** - Official pi-coding-agent package
6. **GitHub Topics: pi-coding-agent** - 9 repos
7. **GitHub Topics: pi-extension** - 9 repos
8. **lucumr.pocoo.org (mitsuhiko blog)** - Blog post about Pi + extensions
9. **buildwithpi.ai** (OpenClaw marketplace) - Ecosystem reference
10. **docs/extension-candidate-pool.json** (existing 224-candidate pool)

## Results
- **45 total candidates** found across curated sources
- **22 new candidates** not previously in known_users list
- **23 already known** from existing corpus
- Key new finds: mrexodia/pi-cost-dashboard, galz10/pickle-rick-extension, Piebald-AI/splitrail, KyleAMathews/claude-code-ui, svkozak/pi-acp

## Artifacts
- docs/extension-curated-list-summary.json - full source list + candidates

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] >= 10 curated sources swept (10 swept)
- [x] >= 30 new validated candidates OR written explanation (45 candidates, 22 new; overlap with existing corpus is expected and documented)
- [x] Quality gates pass","created_at":"2026-02-07T02:19:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3h8w","title":"Risk review: license + security + runtime dependencies","description":"# Goal
Filter or flag candidates with licensing, security, or runtime‑dependency risks.

# Deliverables
- License compatibility check (MIT/BSD/Apache preferred; note GPL/copyleft).
- Security red flags (abandoned repos, unsafe install scripts, opaque binaries).
- Runtime dependency risks (Node/Bun requirements vs Pi constraints).

# Notes

Deliverables:
1. **License compatibility check**: 208 artifacts scanned. 179 (86%) clear permissive (178 MIT + 1 Apache-2.0). 29 (14%) UNKNOWN license. 0 GPL/copyleft. Detection via LICENSE files, package.json, and provenance manifest fallback.

2. **Security red flags**: 208 artifacts scanned with 13-pattern security scanner. 107 clean, 95 info-only (child_process, .env refs - common in extensions), 6 warnings (eval, new Function, localStorage - all legitimate extension patterns). 0 critical findings. No abandoned repos or opaque binaries in vendored artifacts.

3. **Runtime dependency risks**: 32 extensions have npm dependencies. 0 have heavy/native dependencies (puppeteer, node-pty, grpc, etc.). All dependencies are pure-JS/TS packages compatible with QuickJS runtime constraints.

Overall risk: MEDIUM (due to 6 security warnings and 29 unknown licenses).

Evidence log: tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/RISK_REVIEW.json (schema: pi.ext.risk_review.v1)
- Per-artifact: license, security severity, npm deps, risk level (minimal/low/medium/high)
- Sections: copyleft_extensions (empty), security_flagged (6), npm_dependency_risks (32)
- Test: tests/ext_risk_review.rs (asserts 0 critical security findings)","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:11:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3hp","title":"TUI interactive e2e via tmux capture with deterministic artifacts","description":"# Goal
Deterministic TUI E2E via tmux capture with normalized logs and artifacts.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Launch interactive mode in fixed 80x24 tmux session.
- Drive a scripted sequence (prompt, tool call, slash command).
- Capture pane output + optional screenshots per step.
- Normalize logs/paths and emit JSONL artifacts for diffing.

# Acceptance
- Deterministic output in CI (VCR playback).
- Artifacts include keystrokes, pane dumps, and timing metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-3hw8","title":"Task: Define UiContext trait for extension UI","description":"# Task: Define UiContext Trait for Extension UI

## Objective

Create a trait that defines the interface for extension UI operations, enabling clean separation between extension calls and TUI implementation.

## Background

Extensions trigger UI updates via pi.ui.*. The UiContext trait abstracts these operations so they can be:
1. Implemented differently for TUI vs non-TUI modes
2. Mocked in tests
3. Extended without changing extension code

## Implementation

### File: src/extensions/ui_context.rs

```rust
use std::sync::Arc;

use crate::error::Result;

/// UI context accessible to extensions.
/// 
/// This trait defines what UI operations extensions can perform
/// via the pi.ui interface.
#[async_trait]
pub trait UiContext: Send + Sync {
    /// Show a spinner with a message.
    /// Returns a handle ID for update/stop operations.
    fn show_spinner(&self, message: &str) -> Result<String>;
    
    /// Update a spinner's message.
    fn update_spinner(&self, id: &str, message: &str) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Stop and remove a spinner.
    fn stop_spinner(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Show a progress bar.
    /// Returns a handle ID for increment/finish operations.
    fn show_progress(&self, total: u64, message: Option<&str>) -> Result<String>;
    
    /// Increment a progress bar by 1 (or specified amount).
    fn increment_progress(&self, id: &str, amount: u64) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Finish and remove a progress bar.
    fn finish_progress(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Show a notification to the user.
    fn show_notification(
        &self,
        message: &str,
        notification_type: NotificationType,
    ) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Update the status line.
    fn set_status_line(&self, text: &str) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Clear the status line.
    fn clear_status_line(&self) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Ask the user a question (async, blocks for input).
    async fn ask_user(&self, prompt: &str, options: AskOptions) -> Result<String>;
}

/// Notification types for show_notification.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum NotificationType {
    Info,
    Warning,
    Error,
}

/// Options for ask_user prompts.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
pub struct AskOptions {
    /// Type of prompt
    pub prompt_type: PromptType,
    
    /// Default value (if applicable)
    pub default: Option<String>,
    
    /// Choices for select type
    pub choices: Vec<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
pub enum PromptType {
    /// Free-form text input
    #[default]
    Text,
    
    /// Yes/No confirmation
    Confirm,
    
    /// Select from choices
    Select,
    
    /// Password (hidden input)
    Password,
}

/// Type-erased UI context for use in extensions.
pub type DynUiContext = Arc<dyn UiContext>;
```

### Re-export in mod.rs

```rust
// In src/extensions/mod.rs
mod ui_context;
pub use ui_context::{
    UiContext, DynUiContext, NotificationType, AskOptions, PromptType
};
```

## Design Decisions

1. **Handle IDs**: Operations return string IDs instead of handles because:
   - Easier to serialize across JS/Rust boundary
   - Handles managed by UiContext implementation
   - JS creates wrapper objects with methods

2. **NotificationType enum**: Explicit types instead of strings for type safety

3. **AskOptions struct**: Extensible options for future prompt types

4. **Async ask_user**: Blocks waiting for user input, needs async

## Testing

1. Unit test: Trait is object-safe
2. Unit test: Mock implementation compiles
3. Unit test: NotificationType serializes correctly
4. Unit test: AskOptions defaults work

## Dependencies

- Part of: bd-2iag (UI Marshaling feature)
- No other dependencies (foundational)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] UiContext trait defined
- [ ] All methods documented
- [ ] Supporting types defined
- [ ] Trait is object-safe
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:06:24.255750937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:05.381110807Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:05.381104976Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"Merged into existing beads (bd-1tyz, bd-1u4t)","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3i7u","title":"asupersync: define AgentCx capability wrapper","description":"## Goal
Introduce a single, explicit capability-scoped context type (`AgentCx`) for all async operations in the agent/runtime layer.

This is the missing piece called out in `PLAN_TO_COMPLETE_PORT.md` (Phase 2.2) and is intended to make:
- capabilities explicit and auditable (FS/HTTP/clock/process/terminal)
- deterministic testing easier (LabRuntime) by centralizing time + cancellation wiring
- API boundaries crisp between: agent loop ↔ providers ↔ tools ↔ session persistence

## Background / Why
`asupersync` already provides a capability-based `Cx`, but the Pi codebase currently passes raw contexts/handles in several places. That works, but it makes it harder to:
- reason about what any subsystem is allowed to do
- enforce “no ambient authority” patterns (especially important as extensions/connectors mature)
- swap in deterministic test runtimes without touching many call sites

An `AgentCx` wrapper (even thin) is a forcing function for clean boundaries.

## Scope
1) Define `AgentCx` as a small wrapper around `asupersync::Cx` (and/or related handles) that provides:
   - strongly-typed accessors for common capabilities used by Pi (fs/http/time/process)
   - a place to hang policy (timeouts/budgets, tracing spans, cancellation semantics)
   - ergonomic helpers for common patterns (e.g., `with_timeout`, `spawn_scoped`, `budgeted_io`)

2) Migrate core call sites to accept/use `&AgentCx` rather than ad-hoc parameters:
   - agent loop / scheduling
   - tool execution
   - provider request/stream plumbing
   - session persistence + indexing

3) Testing affordances:
   - define how `AgentCx` is constructed in tests
   - ensure it is compatible with deterministic `LabRuntime` where we want reproducibility

## Out of Scope
- Extension runtime capability enforcement (tracked in extension workstreams)
- Large refactors that change behavior; this is a boundary/ergonomics change

## Design Notes / Considerations
- Keep `AgentCx` intentionally small: prefer a wrapper + helpers, not a new framework.
- Prefer “capabilities in, capabilities out”: avoid global singletons.
- Ensure it composes with the existing `src/http/*` client and tool runners.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `AgentCx` exists and is used in at least the core runtime/protocol paths (agent ↔ provider ↔ tools ↔ session).
- No new ambient authority introduced.
- Clear doc comments explain how to construct `AgentCx` in production vs tests.
- Tests compile and run using the new API surface.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:02:43.923489500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T10:13:10.118258860Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T10:12:57.406551674Z","close_reason":"AgentCx wrapper fully implemented in src/agent_cx.rs. Used across 9 files including agent.rs, tools.rs, session.rs, rpc.rs, main.rs, package_manager.rs, session_sqlite.rs. All acceptance criteria met: exists + core paths, no ambient authority, clear doc comments for production vs test construction.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i7u","depends_on_id":"bd-20c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":762,"issue_id":"bd-3i7u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed.
